arguments.query: Xero API documentation developer portal authentication
Xero MCP server model context protocol
Xero developer account signup free API credentials pricing
Xero API documentation REST endpoints accounting
Xero API "MCP" server official Xero AI toolkit
Xero developer account signup free no credit card self serve

researched_category.notes: Xero is a cloud-based accounting platform for small to medium businesses. It provides APIs for accounting, payroll, assets, projects, files, and bank feeds.

one_liner.value: Cloud accounting platform for small to medium businesses that offers REST APIs for invoices, contacts, bank transactions, payroll, reporting, and more.
one_liner.notes: Xero's product page and developer portal confirm it as an online accounting and bookkeeping platform.

methods.notes: Xero primarily uses OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow, PKCE, and client credentials / Custom Connections). Bearer tokens are also supported for the MCP server. The token types page details access tokens (JWT), refresh tokens, and ID tokens.

access.notes: The developer account signup is free. There is a free Starter tier (up to 5 connections, 1,000 API calls/day) and paid tiers (Core, Plus, Advanced, Enterprise) for higher usage. No credit card is required for Starter. The development account page says 'Signing up for a Xero account is free'. The AI Toolkit and MCP server are available on the Starter tier.

summary.value: Xero offers a comprehensive set of public REST APIs: Accounting API (invoices, contacts, bank transactions, reports, journals, etc.), Payroll API (AU, NZ, UK regions), Assets API, Projects API, Files API, and BankFeeds API. Also supports webhooks. Official SDKs exist for Node.js, PHP, Ruby, .NET, Python, Java. The API Explorer and OpenAPI specs are publicly available.
summary.notes: The Accounting API alone covers 30+ resource types. The developer portal lists extensive documentation for each API family.

agent_interface.notes: Xero has an official MCP server published by XeroAPI on GitHub and npm (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server). It also has an 'AI Toolkit' with examples for OpenAI Agents SDK, LangChain, and a CLI tool. The MCP server is listed on the Xero Developer pricing page as available on the free Starter tier.

rationale.value: Xero has a well-documented public REST API, OAuth 2.0 authentication, a free developer tier (Starter) with 5 connections and 1,000 API calls/day, an official MCP server, SDKs for multiple languages, and an AI agent toolkit. A developer can sign up for free, create an app, get API credentials, and build an agent integration today. The main consideration is that scaling beyond 5 connected organizations requires a paid tier.
rationale.notes: The only potential limitation is the 5-connection cap on the free Starter tier and 1,000 API calls/day rate limit, but these are sufficient for development and testing.

s1.quote: Xero Developer: Powering Your Innovation and Growth
s1.title: Xero Developer Platform

s2.quote: Xero uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication. Access tokens are JWT, valid for 30 minutes. Refresh tokens valid for 60 days.
s2.title: Token types — Xero Developer

s3.quote: OAuth 2.0 is a protocol that lets your app access a user’s account without accessing their password.
s3.title: OAuth 2.0 — Xero Developer

s4.quote: This is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation for Xero. It provides a bridge between the MCP protocol and Xero's API.
s4.title: XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server

s5.quote: A collection of examples demonstrating how to build AI agents that integrate with the Xero API using various agentic frameworks and the Xero MCP Server.
s5.title: XeroAPI/xero-agent-toolkit

s6.quote: Starter tier: No charge, max 5 connections, 1000 API calls per day per org. Includes AI toolkit (MCP server, Open AI Agentic SDKs, Prompt library).
s6.title: Pricing — Xero Developer

s7.quote: Accounting API Accounts — RESTful endpoints for accounts, invoices, contacts, bank transactions, reports, etc.
s7.title: Accounting API Accounts — Xero Developer

s8.quote: Signing up for a Xero account is free. Use the demo company (recommended) or start a trial or paid Xero organisation subscription.
s8.title: Development accounts — Xero Developer

s9.quote: Xero API base URLs: api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/ (Accounting), assets.xro/1.0/, projects.xro/2.0/, payroll.xro/2.0/, files.xro/1.0/, bankfeeds.xro/1.0/. OAuth 2.0 required.
s9.title: Xero API Guide 2026: Endpoints, OAuth, Limits & Integration Tips

s10.quote: MCP Server — Securely and seamlessly connect LLMs to Xero data with our open source MCP server. Also CLI, Agentic SDK, Prompt Library.
s10.title: Xero's AI Toolkit — Xero Developer

limitations: Some developer.xero.com pages timed out during fetch; used secondary sources and cached pages instead.
The new API pricing model (Starter/Core/Plus/Advanced/Enterprise) took effect March 2, 2026, meaning the free tier is now limited to 5 connections and 1,000 API calls/day.
